skill.name: ux-researcher-designer
skill.description: UX research and design toolkit for generating research-backed personas and translating user behavior into design implications.
skill.summary: Generate UX personas, archetypes, scenarios, and design implications from structured user research data.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity weak cryptography alerts, but review found no cryptographic code. Those alerts are false positives from UX terms such as design, desktop, mobile-first, and professional design. The skill includes a local Python persona generator, so publication risk is low and should disclose script execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The flagged locations do not use DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or any cryptographic primitive. They contain UX research wording, data field names, and output labels, so the high-severity static alerts are dismissed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of every flagged line found descriptive UX text or ordinary dictionary keys. No hashing, encryption, random security token generation, or credential handling appears at those locations.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Usage
low_findings.description: SKILL.md shows a backticked command example for running the bundled Python script. This is not Ruby or shell backtick execution in code, but users should understand that the skill includes runnable local code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around a usage example, not executable code. The Python entry point prints generated persona output and does not spawn subprocesses or invoke shell commands.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear personas that connect user behavior to design decisions. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code turn research inputs into personas, scenarios, and design implications.
actual_capabilities: Generates personas from structured user data and optional interview insights.
Classifies personas into power user, casual user, business user, or mobile-first archetypes.
Aggregates demographics, behavior patterns, goals, needs, frustrations, and psychographics.
Creates persona scenarios and design implications from observed behavior patterns.
Formats persona results as readable text or JSON output from the Python script.
Provides UX research focus areas for journey mapping, usability testing, synthesis, and validation.
limitations: The script uses heuristic rules, not statistical modeling or validated clustering methods.
The included script uses sample data unless users adapt it for their own inputs.
Journey mapping and usability testing are described as capabilities, but no separate scripts implement them.
Research conclusions depend on the quality, consent status, and representativeness of supplied data.

use_cases.title: Create Product Personas
use_cases.description: Generate practical personas that summarize user needs, frustrations, behaviors, and design implications for a product team.

use_cases.title: Synthesize Interview Themes
use_cases.description: Use interview quotes, goals, motivations, and observed behavior to draft research-backed persona narratives.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Validation
use_cases.description: Translate early user signals into scenarios and assumptions that can guide usability tests or prototype reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Persona
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a UX persona for my product using these user notes. Include goals, frustrations, behaviors, and design implications.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Archetypes
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these user records and identify the most likely persona archetypes. Explain the evidence for each archetype.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Research Into Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize these survey and interview findings into personas, scenarios, design risks, and recommended validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Persona Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these personas for weak evidence, missing segments, bias, and unclear design implications. Recommend improvements.

output_examples.input: User records show daily dashboard use, frequent exports, and frustration with slow reports.
output_examples.output: A business or power user persona with efficiency goals, reporting pain points, and design implications for faster workflows.

output_examples.input: Interview notes mention mobile access, quick tasks, and difficulty finding desktop-only features.
output_examples.output: A mobile-first persona with on-the-go scenarios, touch interaction needs, and feature parity recommendations.

output_examples.input: A team wants to validate a new onboarding flow before launch.

best_practices: Use anonymized research data and remove sensitive personal information before running analysis.
Treat generated personas as hypotheses until validated with users or behavioral evidence.
Pair persona outputs with sample size, confidence level, and clear source notes.
anti_patterns: Do not use the sample data as evidence for a real product decision.
Do not treat heuristic archetypes as statistically proven customer segments.
Do not include private interview details or identifiable user data in prompts or script inputs.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create UX personas, archetypes, scenarios, behavior summaries, and design implications from research inputs.

faq.question: Does it run code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a local Python script for persona generation, so review the script before running it.

faq.question: Can it process real interview data?
faq.answer: It can use interview-style fields, but users should anonymize data and adapt the script for their own format.

faq.question: Does it create journey maps automatically?
faq.answer: The skill describes journey mapping support, but the included script focuses on persona generation.

faq.question: Is the persona output research validated?
faq.answer: No. The output is a structured draft that should be validated with real users and product evidence.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
